name: Moldova
avoid: December–February for grey weather, icy village roads, and fewer winery departure slots
Unplanned Transnistria day trips without cash and passport checks in mind

cities.name: Chișinău
cities.notes: Leafy capital with parks, museums, trolleybuses, wine bars, and the country’s easiest transport hub.

cities.name: Orheiul Vechi
cities.notes: Clifftop archaeological reserve and cave monastery above the Răut River, best paired with a Butuceni village stay.

cities.name: Mileștii Mici
cities.notes: Famous underground wine city south of Chișinău, reached most easily by taxi or pre-booked winery transfer.

cities.name: Tiraspol (Transnistria)
cities.notes: Breakaway-region capital with broad Soviet boulevards, local cognac culture, and a very different feel from the rest of Moldova.

itinerary.sim: Orange Moldova or Moldcell prepaid SIMs are the easiest tourist pick, usually around ₹460–₹920 for 20–50GB; buy them in Chișinău city kiosks, malls, or airport counters before heading to wine country.

Chișinău.name: Chișinău
Chișinău.note: Two days gives you the capital’s museum-and-park core plus a proper winery day trip without turning Chișinău into a rushed transit stop.

days.theme: Central Parks, History Museums & Classic Chișinău Boulevards

activities.name: Stephen the Great Central Park and Alley of Classics walk
Nativity Cathedral and Triumphal Arch stop
National Museum of History of Moldova
National Museum of Ethnography and Natural History
Chișinău trolleybus ride across the center

days.theme: Underground Cellars, Markets & Evening Wine Bars

activities.name: Cricova Winery “Underground City” tour
activities.tip: Reserve ahead; the official weekday 10 a.m. promo is cheaper, while weekends and post-4 p.m. departures add a surcharge.

activities.name: Piața Centrală produce-and-snack browse
Valea Morilor lakeside walk
Organ Hall and Eugen Doga pedestrian street stroll

Orheiul Vechi.name: Orheiul Vechi
Orheiul Vechi.note: One full day is enough for the cave monastery, reserve viewpoints, and a slow Moldovan lunch in Butuceni without backtracking all afternoon.

days.theme: Cave Monastery, Butuceni Village & Răut River Viewpoints

activities.name: Orheiul Vechi museum-reserve and cave monastery access ticket
activities.tip: The reserve officially lists 20 MDL general admission; guided tours and parking are extra if you want more context.

activities.name: Butuceni cliff trail and Răut River panorama walk
Traditional village lanes and ethnographic courtyards in Butuceni
Short taxi or marshrutka hop between Trebujeni and Butuceni viewpoints

Mileștii Mici.name: Mileștii Mici
Mileștii Mici.note: Treat Mileștii Mici as a dedicated tasting day from Chișinău so you can book the right cellar slot and avoid cramming it around intercity transfers.

days.theme: Wine Tunnels, Tasting Halls & South-of-Capital Vineyard Country

activities.name: Mileștii Mici winery visit without tasting
Mileștii Mici “Hai, Noroc!” tasting tour
activities.tip: This 800 MDL tasting is the sweet spot for most travelers; late-afternoon and weekend slots cost about 100 MDL more per person.

activities.name: Taxi from Chișinău to Mileștii Mici and back
Evening glass at Carpe Diem Wine Shop & Bar back in Chișinău

Tiraspol (Transnistria).name: Tiraspol (Transnistria)
Tiraspol (Transnistria).note: One day covers the main Soviet-era avenues and food stops; add a second night only if you want a slower border-region detour or a KVINT-focused visit.

days.theme: Soviet Boulevards, Riverfront Monuments & Transnistria Atmosphere

activities.name: Suvorov Monument and October 25 Street walk
House of Soviets exterior and central square
De Wollant Park and Dniester riverfront stroll
Taxi to Noul Neamț Monastery in Chițcani and back

connections.method: Marshrutka from Chișinău North Bus Station toward Butuceni/Trebujeni, then a short walk or taxi to the reserve (about 1h–1h 30m).
Return marshrutka to Chișinău, then take a taxi or pre-booked winery transfer south to Mileștii Mici (about 2h total with the change).
Head back to Chișinău, then use the train when it is running or the more frequent marshrutka/shared taxi to Tiraspol (about 1h 45m–2h 15m).

extras: May through September is the easiest weather window for this route: vineyard visits run more smoothly, Orheiul Vechi trails are greener, and Tiraspol day trips feel far less bleak than in winter.
Book Cricova and Mileștii Mici at least a day or two ahead; both wineries work on timed tours rather than true walk-ins, and English slots can fill first.
Use Yandex Go or iTaxi in Chișinău instead of street-hail bargaining, and keep some MDL cash for marshrutkas, monastery tickets, and village snack stops.
For Tiraspol, carry your passport and a little extra cash because bank cards can be unreliable and border-control formalities are more cumbersome than elsewhere in Moldova.

Mileștii Mici: Moldova vineyard road near Mileștii Mici
